title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
The Reasons why people think that Term Insurance is a Bad Idea and Why they are Wrong!
Meta Description: Many explanations are given for why people do not get this necessary insurance coverage. Discover why they're wrong, and what to look for in a term plan.
Keywords: term insurance policy, term insurance plan calculator, term insurance benefits
________________________________________________________
Introduction
Life is unpredictable, and no one can predict what the future holds. Hence, you may want to consider getting a term insurance policy to ensure that your family is financially secure in your absence. Term life insurance plans are the most basic, cost-effective, and simple to purchase life insurance policies that cover your family's financial future.
Why Do People Think Term Insurance is a Bad Idea?
Many investors believe that purchasing a term insurance policy is a waste of money. They contend that when the insurance plan matures, the investor will not get any money. They claim that buying a term insurance policy does not provide any financial returns.
So, what kind of insurance policies do these investors often purchase? They could wind up getting endowment or money-back plans, is the answer. Their argument is that these sorts of plans offer something in return for their money. Let’s take a look at why a term insurance policy is a must have kind of insurance product to purchase.
Why should you go for Term Insurance?
Following are some of the term insurance benefits:-
Low premium - For a low premium, you may get a high death benefit or life protection with a term insurance plan. The cost of a term plan is less than that of other insurance policies. It just offers a life insurance policy with no additional benefits or investment component.
Starting early has advantages - The premium you pay for a term plan is determined by your age, policy term and other health factors. The premium will be lower if you acquire a term plan while you are younger.
Lower tenure - A Term Plan may be taken for as little as 10 years. Shorter-term plans are available if you have taken out a house or personal loan. For example, if your house loan term is 10 years, you may take out a 10-year term plan for the same amount as your home loan. This assures that if you pass away during the loan term, your heirs will be able to keep the house by settling the remaining loan sum.
Predetermined intervals – There are term plans that provide increasing life cover at predetermined intervals with a predefined percentage increase. This allows the policyholder to raise his or her life insurance coverage as his or her income rises.
Longer term life insurance - Term policies may be purchased for a long time. In most cases, a term may be taken up to the age of 70 years; however, some firms provide coverage up to the age of 75 years or even whole life.
Fixed premium — Once the insurance provider accepts your policy for a certain premium, the premium will not be revised or changed throughout the policy period. As a result, you can be confident that you will be paying a set yearly sum that will not increase in the future
Riders may be added - Some term plans let you add riders such as accidental death insurance, critical illness insurance, etc. Riders are optional extra benefit that may be purchased for a small cost.
Income Tax Benefits - Under Section 80C of the Income Tax Act 1961, you may claim a deduction of up to Rs 150,000 per year for premiums paid. In addition, under Section 10 (10D) of the Income Tax Act 1961, the death benefit given to your nominee is tax-free.
Simple to purchase - Purchasing a term insurance policy is the simplest thing to do. You may compare the premiums of various term plans on numerous comparison websites and choose the plan that best suits your needs. You may go to the insurance company's website directly and purchase a term plan in a few simple steps to be covered. Remember that term insurance policies purchased online are less expensive than those purchased via business offices or brokers.
Wrapping Up
Term plans appeal to most of us because of the intrinsic advantages of life insurance at the lowest possible price, income tax benefits, and, finally, peace of mind about the protection of our near and dear ones, thanks to increased knowledge about life insurance. When compared to the advantages of a life insurance term plan, the premium you pay is a bargain.
full_pdf_text:
The Reasons why people think that Term Insurance is not a suitable Idea and why they may be wrong! Meta Description: Many explanations are given for why people do not get this insurance coverage. Discover why may be wrong, and what to look for in a term plan. Keywords: term insurance policy, term insurance plan calculator, term insurance benefits ________________________________________________________ Introduction Life is unpredictable, and no one can predict what the future holds. Hence, you may want to consider getting a term insurance policy to ensure that your family is financially secure in your absence. Term life insurance plans are the basic, affordable, and simple to purchase life insurance policies that cover your family's financial future. Why Do People Think Term Insurance is not suitable? It’s a general perception that purchasing a term insurance policy is a waste of money. They contend that when the insurance plan matures, the investor will not get any money. They claim that buying a term insurance policy does not provide any financial returns. So, what kind of insurance policies do these investors often purchase? They could wind up getting endowment or money-back plans, is the answer. Their argument is that these sorts of plans offer something in return for their money. Let’s take a look at why a term insurance policy is a suitable insurance product you may consider purchasing. Why you may go for Term Insurance? Following are some of the term insurance benefits:-
● Affordable premium - For an affordable sum of premium, you may get death benefit or life protection with a term insurance plan as per the policy terms & conditions. The cost of a term plan is less than that of other insurance policies. It just offers a life insurance policy with no additional benefits or investment component.
● Starting early has advantages - The premium you pay for a term plan is determined by your age, policy term and other health factors. The premium will be lower if you acquire a term plan while you are younger.
● Lower tenure - A Term Plan may be taken for as little as 10 years. Shorter-term plans are available if you have taken out a house or personal loan. For example, if your house loan term is 10 years, you may take out a 10-year term plan for the same amount as your home loan. This
assures that if you pass away during the loan term, your nominees will be able to keep the house by settling the remaining loan sum. ● Predetermined intervals – There are term plans that provide increasing life cover at predetermined intervals with a predefined percentage increase. This allows the policyholder to raise his or her life insurance coverage as his or her income rises.
● Longer term life insurance - Term policies may be purchased for a long time. In most cases, a term may be taken up to the age of 70 years; however, some insurance companies provide coverage up to the age of 75 years or even whole life.
● Fixed premium — once the insurance provider accepts your policy for a certain premium, the premium will not be revised or changed throughout the policy period. As a result, you can be confident that you will be paying a set yearly sum that will not increase for that particular policy period ● Riders may be added - Some term plans let you add riders such as accidental death insurance, critical illness insurance, etc. Riders are optional extra benefit that may be purchased by paying a nominal additional premium over and above the base insurance premium. .
● Income Tax Benefits - Under Section 80C of the Income Tax Act 1961, you may claim a deduction of up to Rs. 150,000 per year for premiums paid. In addition, under Section 10 (10D) of the Income Tax Act 1961, the death benefit given to your nominee is tax-free. The said tax benefits are subject to the provisions stated in the Income Tax Act 1961.
● Simple to purchase - Purchasing a term insurance policy is the simplest thing to do. You may compare the premiums of various term plans and choose the plan that suits your needs. You may go to the insurance company's website directly and purchase a term plan in a few simple steps. Remember that term insurance policies purchased online are at times less expensive than those purchased via business offices or brokers. Wrapping Up Term plans appeal to most of us because of the intrinsic advantages of life insurance at an affordable price, income tax benefits, and, peace of mind about the financial protection of our near and dear ones, thanks to increased knowledge about life insurance.
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article is not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions.
